subject: REVIEW — cognitive-tempo model: LC-NE system provides strongest mapping

body.summary: Reviewed 4 candidate analogies. The Locus Coeruleus-Norepinephrine (LC-NE) system (Aston-Jones & Cohen, 2005) provides the strongest mapping because it directly modulates processing mode based on environmental demands.

theoretical_grounding.rationale: The LC-NE system regulates the exploitation/exploration tradeoff through a gain parameter. This maps directly to model tier selection: low gain favors routine exploitation (haiku), phasic NE bursts drive focused attention (sonnet), and high tonic NE supports exploratory processing of novel material (opus).

tier_mapping.haiku: Tonic low-NE state — routine exploitation, well-practiced responses, minimal resource allocation
tier_mapping.sonnet: Phasic NE bursts — focused attention on moderately demanding tasks, balanced resource allocation
tier_mapping.opus: High tonic NE — exploratory mode for novel/complex material, maximum resource allocation

body.existing_infrastructure: The neuromodulatory mesh spec (docs/neuromodulatory-mesh-spec.md, Session 90) already models this pattern — mesh.alert topic carries the NE-analog signal.

decision_function.description: The cognitive-tempo API endpoint should compute the recommended tier from four input signals combined into a gain estimate.
input_signals: Message complexity heuristic (word count, claim count, domain novelty)
Context pressure (active session count, pending gates)
decision_function.routing_logic: If urgency=immediate OR complexity exceeds threshold, recommend opus. If routine ACK/status message, recommend haiku. Otherwise, recommend sonnet.

action_gate.gate_condition: operations-agent integrates cognitive-tempo decision function into meshd spawn path
